title: Controlling (CO)
learning_objectives: Understand the purpose of SAP Controlling (CO).
Learn how costs are planned, monitored and analyzed.
Understand cost centers, profit centers and internal orders.
Identify commonly used SAP CO Transaction Codes.
Learn how CO integrates with other SAP modules.

introduction.title: What is SAP CO?
introduction.content: SAP Controlling (CO) is the module used for planning, monitoring and controlling business costs. It provides management with detailed information about operational expenses, profitability and resource utilization to support better decision-making.

business_purpose.title: Why is SAP CO Used?
business_purpose.content: Organizations need to know where money is being spent and whether operations are profitable. SAP CO helps monitor production costs, departmental expenses, product profitability and overall business performance.

major_components.name: Cost Center
major_components.description: Represents a department or unit where costs are incurred.

major_components.name: Profit Center
major_components.description: Represents a business unit whose profitability is measured.

major_components.name: Internal Order
major_components.description: Tracks costs for a specific project, event or activity.

major_components.name: Product Costing
major_components.description: Calculates the production cost of finished products.

major_components.name: Profitability Analysis (CO-PA)
major_components.description: Analyzes profit earned from products, customers or markets.

integration.purpose: Receives financial postings for cost analysis.

business_example.title: Example
business_example.content: A stainless steel production order consumes raw materials, electricity and labor. SAP CO collects all associated costs, compares them with planned costs and generates reports showing cost variances and product profitability.

best_practices: Integrate cost planning with production planning.
common_mistakes: Posting expenses to incorrect Cost Centers.
